title: Gros, Napoleon Bonaparte Visiting the Plague-Stricken in Jaffa
description: Test your knowledge on this object.
topic_path: Gros, Napoleon Bonaparte Visiting the Plague-Stricken in Jaffa

assessment_items.question_content:
Which term best describes the function of Gros's painting *Napoleon Bonaparte Visiting the Plague-Stricken in Jaffa*? 



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2dc07962d87c8903e9abb5058ebf30aa.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Gros created an idealized account of an historical event, when Napoleon visited diseased soldiers at Jaffa.

This painting served as propaganda for Napoleon's government by eliminating the atrocities of the historical event.  

**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1800-1848-industrial-revolution-i/romanticism-in-france/v/gros--napoleon-bonaparte-visiting-the-pest-house-in-jaffa--1804)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which elements distinguish this painting as an early example of French Romanticism?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2dc07962d87c8903e9abb5058ebf30aa.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: In keeping with the tradition of French Neoclassicism, Gros modeled Napoleon on the Apollo Belvedere, a classical sculpture from antiquity.

This painting shows the shift toward Romanticism in the inclusion of Islamic architectural details and other exotic subject matter.  


**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1800-1848-industrial-revolution-i/romanticism-in-france/v/gros--napoleon-bonaparte-visiting-the-pest-house-in-jaffa--1804)**

choices.content: The monumental scale of the painting
The exotic details and Islamic architecture

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following does NOT contribute to Napoleon's heroic depiction and divine status?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2dc07962d87c8903e9abb5058ebf30aa.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Gros uses dramatic lighting to draw attention to Napoleon and highlight his important presence in the painting.

The foreign setting contributes to the painting's exotic appeal, but Napoleon's divine presence is demonstrated through his highlighted position, classical posture, and apparent immunity to contagion.  

**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1800-1848-industrial-revolution-i/romanticism-in-france/v/gros--napoleon-bonaparte-visiting-the-pest-house-in-jaffa--1804)**

choices.content: He is modeled on the Apollo Belvedere.
He is shown surrounded with light.
He is shown in a foreign setting.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which detail of the real story of Napoleon's visit to the plague stricken did Gros NOT alter in his painting? **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2dc07962d87c8903e9abb5058ebf30aa.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: At the time of his visit to Jaffa, Napoleon was not yet Emperor of France.

While fighting the British, some of the French soldiers were infected by the plague and housed in an Islamic mosque in Jaffa.


**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1800-1848-industrial-revolution-i/romanticism-in-france/v/gros--napoleon-bonaparte-visiting-the-pest-house-in-jaffa--1804)**

choices.content: The sick were housed in an Islamic mosque.
Soldiers were forced to drink laudanum.
Napoleon's status as Emperor among the troops.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Gros's use of light in this painting demonstrates what influence on the artist?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Gros's painting reflects the theatrical influence of Baroque art in the dramatic lighting and stage-like setting of Islamic architecture. 

**[Watch this video to review.](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1800-1848-industrial-revolution-i/romanticism-in-france/v/gros--napoleon-bonaparte-visiting-the-pest-house-in-jaffa--1804)**
